name: verifiability-lens
description: An automatic helper that sorts any plan/claim/result into verifiable (A) vs guess (B) vs can't-tell (U), then hands you only the important, actionable, fully-contextualized decisions — absorbing the rest (minor suppressed, settleable auto-resolved + logged). Detection: a read-only verifiability-lens agent classifies work by whether a cheap accurate check exists; class is capability-relative; a U dressed as A (false-clean) is the failure it catches. Delivery: a surfacing triage (auto-resolve | escalate | suppress) tuned by a recipient profile picks up the user's slack — hard rule, never a context-less decision; auto-resolutions always logged. Fires automatically: a Stop hook (opt-in, OFF by default) blocks each classify-worthy turn and runs the lens in-session before yielding, with a fire-exactly-once loop guard (same mechanism as essense-autopilot). Also a /verifiability manual trigger. Carries a hook — install separately from the mk-cc-all bundle.
